variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_sig/sig.py — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each case: line A is N (>= 1). Line B is N space-separated floats. For each case, output the element-wise sigmoid σ(x) = 1/(1 + e^-x) for each value, formatted as `%.9f`, space-separated on one line. Use the numerically stable form: for x >= 0, 1/(1+exp(-x)); for x < 0, exp(x)/(1+exp(x)) — so sigmoid(1000) doesn't overflow and sigmoid(-1000) doesn't underflow to NaN. Pure Python; no numpy.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_sig; python3 sig.py < input.txt > out.txt; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_sig/sig.go (package main). Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each case: line A is N (>= 1). Line B is N space-separated floats. For each case, output the element-wise sigmoid σ(x) = 1/(1 + e^-x) for each value, formatted as `%.9f`, space-separated on one line. Use the numerically stable form: for x >= 0, 1/(1+exp(-x)); for x < 0, exp(x)/(1+exp(x)) — so sigmoid(1000) doesn't overflow and sigmoid(-1000) doesn't underflow to NaN. Compile with `go build -o sig sig.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_sig; go build -o sig sig.go && ./sig < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_sig/sig.c — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each case: line A is N (>= 1). Line B is N space-separated floats. For each case, output the element-wise sigmoid σ(x) = 1/(1 + e^-x) for each value, formatted as `%.9f`, space-separated on one line. Use the numerically stable form: for x >= 0, 1/(1+exp(-x)); for x < 0, exp(x)/(1+exp(x)) — so sigmoid(1000) doesn't overflow and sigmoid(-1000) doesn't underflow to NaN. Compile with `gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o sig sig.c -lm`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_sig; gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o sig sig.c -lm && ./sig < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_sig/sig.rs — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each case: line A is N (>= 1). Line B is N space-separated floats. For each case, output the element-wise sigmoid σ(x) = 1/(1 + e^-x) for each value, formatted as `%.9f`, space-separated on one line. Use the numerically stable form: for x >= 0, 1/(1+exp(-x)); for x < 0, exp(x)/(1+exp(x)) — so sigmoid(1000) doesn't overflow and sigmoid(-1000) doesn't underflow to NaN. Idiomatic Rust 2021+; compile with `rustc -O sig.rs -o sig`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_sig; rustc -O sig.rs -o sig 2>/dev/null && ./sig < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_sig/sig.zig — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each case: line A is N (>= 1). Line B is N space-separated floats. For each case, output the element-wise sigmoid σ(x) = 1/(1 + e^-x) for each value, formatted as `%.9f`, space-separated on one line. Use the numerically stable form: for x >= 0, 1/(1+exp(-x)); for x < 0, exp(x)/(1+exp(x)) — so sigmoid(1000) doesn't overflow and sigmoid(-1000) doesn't underflow to NaN. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Compile with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast sig.zig`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_sig; zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast sig.zig && ./sig < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
